releases.name: Eden v0.2.1
releases.body:
## Changelog

This is a hotfix release for 0.2.0, addressing some crashes and bugs that occurred in certain situations. CPU and graphics performance will remain largely the same.

This release does *not* use SDL3, and remains on SDL2.

# Universal

- The Windows auto updater now accounts for the current build ID and will properly fetch the corresponding build to your currently installed Eden
- Fixed crashes and errors that could occur when large amounts of games and external content files were present
- Fixed crashes on 12th-gen Intel CPUs
- Fixed MK8DX freeze when the native controller applet was used
- Fixed a rare case of weird noises playing on shutdown
- Fixed mods that expect a real value out of `DefaultDataSpace`
- Fixed some minor issues with emulated controller thread contention

# Internal

- Fixed several build errors:
  - Newer Xbyak removed Xeon Phi features
  - Unused variable errors in NCE
  - Removed `httplib` version restrictions

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

### Room Executables

These are statically linked Linux executables for the `eden-room` binary.

- [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/eden-room-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
- [x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/eden-room-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Android-v0.2.1-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Android-v0.2.1-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Android-v0.2.1-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-macOS-v0.2.1.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-v0.2.1.torrent)

releases.name: Eden v0.2.0
releases.body:
## Changelog

This is the first anniversary of Eden's inception, on May 10, 2025. It has been quite a hectic journey thus far, from drama, to server nukes, to DMCAs, to sysadmin nightmares, to exotic hardware/OS ports, to constant threats from all angles. Yet still, we survive, with countless improvements, bug fixes, performance enhancements, features, and so much more to show for it.

Thank you all so, so much for the constant support in the face of continuous, unending challenges and difficulties. Here's to many more years of Eden, and for the time being, enjoy 0.2.0 as your anniversary gift!

You are encouraged to read the [detailed changelog](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/v0.2.0-rc2...v0.2.0).

# Universal

- New 1st anniversary icon
- Firmware 22.0 compatibility
- Removed the separate logging thread
  - Now all logging is done in-place
- Stubbed GetFriendListForViewerV2
  - Qlaunch will no longer crash when viewing friends
- Various changes to make the finalized assembly of the binary (host executable) be cleaner and reduce indirection
  - Notably, this includes new C++ guidelines for structuring code and make compilation better
  - This doesn't affect code generated by JIT (i.e the code of the programs being ran under the emulator), only the emulator itself
- Fix Qlauncher crashing when opened twice
- Built-in auto-updater
- Common global random number generator
- Fix crashes for shared_memory
  - Fixes Tomodachi Life (boot)
- Updated firmware constants to 22.0, Atmosphere to 1.10.2
- Improve TLS fetching performance on thread_data

# Renderer

- Global maintainance Vulkan fixes and improvements for all platforms
  - Notably fixes performance and accuracy issues across the board
- Simplified texture cache GC
- Fixed index scaling for descriptors
- Mark sampled image descriptors indices as non uniform
- Handle dynamic texture descriptor strides
- Expose GPU-async for other platforms
- SPIRV-Opt has been nuked
  - This means no more SPIRV optimizations
  - ...many of which were just placebo and made no change other than making shader comp significantly slower
- Improved Lanczos filtering shader
  - Unrolling loops causes a slight improvement
- Fix CBuf get/set for composite types
- Set high Z-index to fix keyboard display
  - Mainly affects TL:LTD
- Fix Maxwell3D processing always marking dirty flags
- Vertex buffer uses static storage

# Desktop

- MSVC builds have been dropped in favour of clang-cl
  - This is mainly due to GitHub runners updating MSVC 2022 to MSVC 2026, which contains compiler bugs
- Fix mod manager bugs
- Remove fastmem ban for FreeBSD
  - Issue was with the vm_reclaim_contig in kernel when using improper flags, fixed on 15
- Fix FFmpeg not detecting VA-API on FreeBSD

# Android

- Better QCOM and Snapdragon compatibility and performance
- Added an option to control the number of Vulkan workers on Android (from 4 to 8)
- Enable legacy rescale pass on Android
  - This fixes games like LM3

# Dynarmic

- Various improvements to the dispatcher, decoder and IR constructor
  - Improves JIT latency
- Removed redundant ARM decode matcher indirection
- Implement VectorMin/Max{S,U}64 emitters
  - Makes Sonic Crossworlds, and many more games, be able to boot on macOS and WoA
- Proper MWAIT push/pop handling
  - This fixes specific CPUs who have such instruction
  - Prevents crashes
- GDB improvements
- Removed VTable bounce hack
  - This was needed for PKZA, but it's no longer necesary
- mcl dependency removed

# Internal

- Allow FFmpeg cross compilation
  - This should make porting significantly easier (OpenSSL pending)
- More documentation on debugging, compiling and such
- Fixes when using XCode CMake generator
- Fixes for RISC-V builds
  - This is not production-ready yet, but it compiles now.
- Remove shader directory regeneration
  - This breaks support with *BSD make, but fixes regeneration of the shader files
- Fix liquid glass icon on MacOS

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2, with patches for Game/Desktop mode support |
| ROG Ally X | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4, with patches for Game/Desktop mode support |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

### Room Executables

These are statically linked Linux executables for the `eden-room` binary.

- [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/eden-room-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
- [x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/eden-room-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

releases.name: Eden v0.2.0-rc2
releases.body:
## Changelog

This release contains a myriad of performance improvements and graphical fixes, alongside general UI and UX improvements, internal improvements, and much more. There are a **ton** of changes--far too many to go over here--so as always, go through the [full changelog](<https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/v0.2.0-rc1...v0.2.0-rc2>) yourself for more details.

## Universal

- Fixed Breath of the Wild 1.9.0 not displaying the correct game name
- JIT and memory performance improvements
- VSync is now disabled if Turbo Mode is active
- Fixed audio in Pokemon Fire Red/Leaf Green

## Renderer

- Fixed invisible mice textures in Luigi's Mansion 3
- Fixed graphical issues and crashes on older Qualcomm GPUs
- Fixed various vertex explosions and color artifacting
  * This primarily affects games such as Mario Kart, Luigi's Mansion 3, and the Zelda games.
- Fixed shader bugs on newer AMD drivers on Windows
- Fixed Skyward Sword HD eye glitch

## Desktop

- Added a basic frametime and FPS overlay
  * This can be enabled with Ctrl+V
- Fixed importing of RomFS mods from zip/folder
- Fixed the game list `Title` column starting out super small by default
- Added tooltips to the game grid, showing current game version & playtime
- Added the Applets tab to the per-game configuration
- Mods can now be deleted or opened in the file manager from the per-game configuration menu
- The GPU selector box now shows its driver
- Fixed Applets tab titlebar cutoff
- Added back the IR camera (if `YUZU_USE_QT_MULTIMEDIA` is on)

## Android

- Fixed bugs and graphical oddities with game pause/resume

## Internal

- FFmpeg and SDL have been updated, which fixes a Vulkan VP9 decoding bug and adds gyro and rumble support to 8BitDo controllers respectively.
- MbedTLS has been replaced with OpenSSL. This generally improves performance, especially on custom optimized OpenSSL builds
- Added support for Polish and Thai language packs
- Fastmem and NCE are automatically disabled on Linux systems that don't support it
- Several configuration matrices have been removed:
  * `YUZU_USE_CPM`
  * `ENABLE_SDL2` (always on except for Android)
  * `ENABLE_OPENSSL` (required now)

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

**Compilers**

- **MSVC**: The default compiler for Windows. This is the most stable experience, but may lack in performance compared to any of the following alternatives.
- **GCC**: The standard GNU compiler; this is the default for Linux and will provide the most stable experience.
- **PGO**: These are built with Clang, and use PGO. PGO (profile-guided optimization) uses data from prior compilations
to determine the \"hotspots\" found within the codebase. Using these hotspots,
it can allocate more resources towards these heavily-used areas, and thus generally see improved performance to the tune of ~10-50%,
depending on the specific game, hardware, and platform. Do note that additional instabilities may occur.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | GCC | PGO | Notes |
|------------|-----|-----|-------|
| amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| 
| Legacy amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness)
| Steam Deck | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 2, with additional patches for SteamOS
| ROG Ally X | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 4

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!),
and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.

releases.name: Eden v0.2.0-rc1
releases.body:
## Changelog

This release contains several new features and UX improvements, notably:
- Updates and DLC without installing to NAND
- A grid and icon-only view for desktop
- Direct mod import from a folder or zip file
- Turbo and Slow mode toggles

And, of course, your usual dose of performance improvements, bug fixes, and regression fixes from earlier versions.
If you wish to see the *full* changelog, check out <https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/v0.1.1...v0.2.0-rc1> as always.

Enjoy, and happy (late, for our more eastward friends) Valentine's Day!

## Universal

- Updates and DLCs can now be selected and used without having to install them to NAND
  * On Android, this is done in Settings -> Manage Game Folders -> \"+\" -> External Content Folder
  * On Desktop, this is done in Settings -> General -> External Content
- Added configurable speed-up and slow-down hotkeys
  * On Android, this is located in the Quick Settings menu
- Some frequently-used sets and maps have been replaced with a faster implementation
- The web token and username are now generated automatically
- The console and battery serial are now generated automatically
  * This may safeguard against adversarial mods or programs attempting to determine if they are running on an emulator.
- Improved JIT caching and page performance
- Fixed Super Mario All Stars game switching
- Fixed booting on some games that require an attached microSD card
- Fixed regressions in Sonic x Shadow and NSMBU Deluxe
- Fixed flickering and performance in Luigi's Mansion 3 (again)

## Renderer

- Improved rendering performance on Mali-based chipsets
- Improvements to macOS/MoltenVK surface handling
- Added an option to specify the target frame pacing
  * This improves frame pacing in situations where the monitor refresh rate and game FPS don't match.
- Fixed persistent ExtendedDynamicState-related graphical glitches that would occur on Windows

## Desktop

- Added a grid view
  * Game names can be turned off for a TV-like icon-only view
  * This can be accessed in the View menu
- Added the ability to import mods and cheats from a folder or zip file
- The game list now scrolls smoothly with a mouse wheel, and can be horizontally shift-scrolled as well
- Fixed Nintendo controllers having incorrect axis ordering

## Android

- Removed the now-redundant LRU Cache option
- Added a button to the home screen to launch the Switch home menu
- Added profile management, ported from the desktop implementation
- Fixed some issues with Quick Settings
- Added GPU debug logging

## Internal

- The minimum Vulkan version is now 1.4.321
- The build system is more or less stable now, and changes thereof should slow down for the foreseeable future.

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

**Compilers**

- **MSVC**: The default compiler for Windows. This is the most stable experience, but may lack in performance compared to any of the following alternatives.
- **GCC**: The standard GNU compiler; this is the default for Linux and will provide the most stable experience.
- **PGO**: These are built with Clang, and use PGO. PGO (profile-guided optimization) uses data from prior compilations
	to determine the "hotspots" found within the codebase. Using these hotspots,
	it can allocate more resources towards these heavily-used areas, and thus generally see improved performance to the tune of ~10-50%,
	depending on the specific game, hardware, and platform. Do note that additional instabilities may occur.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | GCC | PGO | Notes |
|------------|-----|-----|-------|
| amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| 
| Legacy amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness)
| Steam Deck | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 2, with additional patches for SteamOS
| ROG Ally X | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 4
| aarch64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))|

## Windows

releases.name: Eden v0.1.1
releases.body:
# Changelog

This is a hotfix release that addresses several UI and platform-specific bugs present in the 0.1.0 release, alongside some bonus fixes and UX improvements.

- Fixed several bugs with the profile manager, such as images not being able to be set, UUIDs not matching folder names, only being able to add 4 profiles, and more
  * Alongside this, the profile manager's UX has been completely overhauled.
- Fixed some macOS-specific graphics and UI bugs
- Fixed Big Brain Academy on Linux
- Fixed blurry game images on the game list when using a scaled display on Windows
- Added back the ability to toggle Fast GPU time during gameplay
- Fixed the home menu not being usable with NCE
- Fixed the overlay applet causing crashes
- Fixed games immediately crashing on Windows 11 
- Fixed builds with OpenSSL enabled but the web service disabled

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.

| MinGW PGO | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-mingw-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | [arm64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-mingw-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | 

## Android

| Build  | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| [Android Standard](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-standard.apk) | Single APK for all supported Android devices (most users should use this) |
| [Android x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-chromeos.apk) | For devices running Chrome/FydeOS, AVD emulators, or certain Intel Atom Android devices. |
| [Android Optimized](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-optimized.apk) | For any Android device that has Frame Generation or any other per-device feature |
| [Android Legacy](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-legacy.apk) | For Adreno A6xx and other older GPUs |

## macOS

macOS comes in a tarballed app. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [macOS](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-macOS-v0.1.1.tar.gz) | For Apple Silicon (M1, M2, etc)|

## Source

Contains all source code, submodules, and CPM cache at the time of release.
This can be extracted with `tar xf Eden-Source-v0.1.1.tar.zst`.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [tar.zst](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Source-v0.1.1.tar.zst) | Source as a zstd-compressed tarball (Windows: use Git Bash or MSYS2) |

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-5219b9f3d2-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Windows-5219b9f3d2-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Android-5219b9f3d2-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Android-5219b9f3d2-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-Android-5219b9f3d2-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-macOS-5219b9f3d2.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1781122546.5219b9f3d2/Eden-5219b9f3d2.torrent)

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.
